meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Russia attacks Odesa, claims Ukraine hit Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant
items.summary: Ukrainian attack on captured Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant kills one worker, according to managers installed by Russia.

items.title: North Korea opens museum commemorating troops killed fighting for Russia
items.summary: Russia and North Korea deepen ties with plans for military cooperation and a symbolic memorial museum in Pyongyang.

items.title: The war on Iran is eroding nuclear non-proliferation
items.summary: US-Israeli attacks on Iran under the pretext of ending a nuclear weapons programme are damaging confidence in the NPT.

items.title: Questions over Trump’s security after shooting at press gala
items.summary: After gunshots echoed at this year’s Correspondents' dinner, security measures for President Trump came under scrutiny.

items.title: Iran’s top diplomat in Russia as Tehran intensifies efforts to end the war
items.summary: Pakistan and Oman remain involved in backchannel efforts to prevent a return to open US-Israel war on Iran.

items.title: War and neglect fuel deadly measles epidemic in Sudan’s Darfur
items.summary: Measles kills 70 in East Darfur's Labado in a few weeks as healthcare collapse leaves families without medicines.

items.title: ‘I have to protect them’: The man guarding Mauritania’s rare Islamic books
items.summary: Chinguetti was once a popular desert library town attracting scholars from the Muslim world to its precious collections.

items.title: ACL injury ends Xavi Simons’ World Cup 2026 dream with the Netherlands
items.summary: Dutch midfielder suffers an ACL injury to his right knee, ending his chances of playing in the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 59 amid diplomatic push to end conflict?
items.summary: Iran discusses diplomatic solutions with regional partners as Trump says Iran could telephone if it wants to talk.

items.title: Zaragoza goalkeeper punches player, sparks brawl in La Liga 2 derby match
items.summary: Real Zaragoza's Esteban Andrada faces a ban for punching Huesca's Jorge Pulido after being shown a red card.

items.title: King Charles US visit: What to know about the itinerary, Congress address
items.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla’s visit comes amid US–UK tensions and the 250th anniversary of US independence.

items.title: Activists accused of raiding Israeli weapons factory face trial in Germany
items.summary: Five European nationals known as the 'Ulm Five' are said to have targeted a site linked to Elbit Systems.

items.title: Rockets thrash LeBron’s Lakers; Wembanyama triumphant on Spurs’ return
items.summary: Spurs beat Trail Blazers 114–93 series lead as DPOY Wembanyama returns from concussion protocol.

items.title: Taiwan court hands down jail terms in TSMC trade secrets case
items.summary: Ex-Tokyo Electron employee sentenced to 10 years, four other defendants receive between 10 months and six years.

items.title: From Australia to Vietnam, the Iran war is fuelling demand for EVs
items.summary: Electric vehicle sales are surging in many markets as conflict drives up the cost of running petrol and diesel vehicles.

items.title: Satellite images show scale of Israeli destruction of south Lebanon towns
items.summary: Satellite images taken reveal the Israeli military's massive scale of damage to towns in south Lebanon.

items.title: Oil prices rise amid stalled US-Iran peace talks
items.summary: Brent crude rises more than 2 percent after Washington and Tehran fail to hold second round of talks in Pakistan.

items.title: Iran war live: Araghchi to meet Putin; Strait of Hormuz remains shut
items.summary: Iran's foreign minister heads to Russia as Trump says Iranian leaders can call on the phone if they want to talk.

items.title: Video: Israeli ‘ceasefire’ violations on the rise in Gaza
items.summary: Israel has violated the so-called Gaza “ceasefire” more than 2,400 times. Al Jazeera’s Hala Al Shami explains.

items.title: Iran’s foreign minister leaves Pakistan, heads to Russia for more talks
items.summary: Abbas Araghchi will speak with 'senior officials' in Moscow, Iran's Foreign Ministry says.

items.title: Israeli settler attacks Hebron activist’s home as soldier looks on
items.summary: An Israeli settler was filmed throwing rocks and trying to break into the home of Palestinian activist Issa Amro.

items.title: What’s driving attacks against gov’t and Russian forces in Mali?
items.summary: Opponents, including al-Qaeda-linked group, join forces.

items.title: Bomb attack on Colombia highway kills 19 ahead of election
items.summary: Highway bombing in Colombia’s Cauca kills 19, injures dozens amid a surge in violence ahead of next month’s election.

items.title: Former Israeli PMs Bennett, Lapid unite to challenge Netanyahu in elections
items.summary: The two politicians' alliance is aimed at uniting a fragmented opposition against current PM Benjamin Netanyahu.

items.title: Has Iran exposed the limits of what US can achieve by force?
items.summary: Political scientist Vali Nasr argues that US and Israeli military options ‘have come up short'.
